paths.@transcodes-guard-private/stepup-core: Public code must not import @transcodes-guard-private/* directly. The GateBackend DI boundary is in place — call getGateBackend() from @transcodes-guard/gate-contract instead.
paths.@transcodes-guard-private/transcodes-mcp: Public code must not import @transcodes-guard-private/* directly. Backend tools are registered through GateBackend.registerBackendTools() — call getGateBackend() from @transcodes-guard/gate-contract instead.
paths.@transcodes-guard-private/danger-rules: Public code must not import @transcodes-guard-private/* directly. Tool rules are exposed through the GateBackend tool-rule methods — call getGateBackend() from @transcodes-guard/gate-contract instead.
paths.@transcodes-guard-private/gate-backend: Public code must not import the private gate backend directly. The only allowed import sites are the plugin backend.ts seams (public/plugins/*/backend.ts, exempted via biome overrides); everything else calls getGateBackend() from @transcodes-guard/gate-contract.

paths.@transcodes-guard-private/stepup-core: Even the backend.ts seam may only import @transcodes-guard-private/gate-backend — gate logic stays behind the GateBackend interface.
paths.@transcodes-guard-private/transcodes-mcp: Even the backend.ts seam may only import @transcodes-guard-private/gate-backend — backend tools are registered via GateBackend.registerBackendTools().
paths.@transcodes-guard-private/danger-rules: Even the backend.ts seam may only import @transcodes-guard-private/gate-backend — tool rules are exposed through the GateBackend tool-rule methods.
